18	from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction_policy import CreateEvictionPolicy
19	from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
20	    EvictionAction,
21	    EvictionDestination,
22	)
23	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l1_manager import L1Manager
24	from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.base import L2AdapterInterface
25	from lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_controller import StorageControllerInterface
26	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event import Event, EventType
27	from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event_bus import get_event_bus
28	
29	if TYPE_CHECKING:
30	    # First Party
31	    from lmcache.v1.distributed.quota_manager import QuotaManager
32	
33	logger = init_logger(__name__)
34	
35	
36	class EvictionController(StorageControllerInterface):
37	    """
38	    Abstract base class for eviction controllers.
39	
40	    Provides the shared eviction loop structure: background thread and stop
41	    flag. Subclasses implement eviction_loop and execute_eviction_action
42	    for their specific tier (L1 or L2).
43	    """
44	
45	    def __init__(self):
46	        self._stop_flag = threading.Event()
47	        self._thread = threading.Thread(
48	            target=self.eviction_loop,
49	            daemon=True,
50	        )
51	
52	    def start(self):
53	        logger.info("Starting %s...", self.__class__.__name__)
54	        self._thread.start()
55	
56	    def stop(self):
57	        self._stop_flag.set()
58	        self._thread.join()
59	
60	    @abstractmethod
61	    def report_status(self) -> dict:
62	        """Return a status dict for this controller.
63	
64	        The child class needs to override this function to report
65	        controller-specific health and configuration information.
66	        """
67	        pass
68	
69	    @abstractmethod
70	    def eviction_loop(self):
71	        """Run the eviction loop.
72	
73	        The child class needs to override this function to implement
74	        internal eviction controlling logic.
75	        """
76	        pass
77	
78	    @abstractmethod
79	    def execute_eviction_action(self, action: EvictionAction):
80	        """Execute a single eviction action.
81	
82	        The child class needs to override this function to implement
83	        internal eviction controlling logic.
84	        """
85	        pass
86	
87	
88	class L1EvictionController(EvictionController):
89	    """
90	    Eviction controller for L1 cache.
91	
92	    Uses an L1EvictionPolicy bridge to keep the eviction policy up-to-date
93	    with L1 manager events, and periodically triggers eviction based on
94	    L1 memory usage.
95	    """
96	
97	    def __init__(
98	        self,
99	        l1_manager: L1Manager,
100	        eviction_config: EvictionConfig,
101	    ):
102	        super().__init__()
103	        self._eviction_config = eviction_config
104	        self._eviction_policy = CreateEvictionPolicy(eviction_config)
105	        self._l1_manager = l1_manager
106	        self._listener = L1EvictionPolicy(self._eviction_policy)
107	        self._l1_manager.register_listener(self._listener)
108	        self._event_bus = get_event_bus()
109	
110	    def report_status(self) -> dict:
111	        return {
112	            "is_healthy": self._thread.is_alive(),
113	            "thread_alive": self._thread.is_alive(),
114	            "eviction_policy": self._eviction_config.eviction_policy,
115	            "trigger_watermark": self._eviction_config.trigger_watermark,
116	            "eviction_ratio": self._eviction_config.eviction_ratio,
117	        }
118	
119	    def _publish_skipped(self, usage: float, watermark: float) -> None:
120	        """Publish a below-watermark loop tick (no eviction this cycle)."""
121	        self._event_bus.publish(
122	            Event(
123	                event_type=EventType.L1_EVICTION_LOOP_TICK,
124	                metadata={
125	                    "usage": usage,
126	                    "watermark": watermark,
127	                    "triggered": False,
128	                },
129	            )
130	        )
131	
132	    def _publish_triggered(self, usage: float, watermark: float) -> None:
133	        """Publish an above-watermark loop tick (eviction policy ran)."""
134	        self._event_bus.publish(
135	            Event(
136	                event_type=EventType.L1_EVICTION_LOOP_TICK,
137	                metadata={
138	                    "usage": usage,
139	                    "watermark": watermark,
140	                    "triggered": True,
141	                },
142	            )
143	        )
144	
145	    def eviction_loop(self):
146	        watermark = self._eviction_config.trigger_watermark
147	        eviction_ratio = self._eviction_config.eviction_ratio
148	
149	        while not self._stop_flag.is_set():
150	            time.sleep(1)
151	            used_bytes, total_bytes = self._l1_manager.get_memory_usage()
152	            usage = 0 if total_bytes == 0 else used_bytes / total_bytes
153	            if usage < watermark:
154	                logger.debug(
155	                    "L1 memory usage %.2f below watermark %.2f; skipping eviction.",
156	                    usage,
157	                    watermark,
158	                )
159	                self._publish_skipped(usage, watermark)
160	                continue
161	
162	            logger.info(
163	                "L1 memory usage %.2f above watermark %.2f; triggering eviction.",
164	                usage,
165	                watermark,
166	            )
167	            actions = self._eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(
168	                eviction_ratio,
169	                key_eligible_filter=self._l1_manager.is_key_evictable,
170	            )
171	            for action in actions:
172	                self.execute_eviction_action(action)
173	            self._publish_triggered(usage, watermark)
174	
175	    def execute_eviction_action(self, action: EvictionAction):
176	        if action.destination == EvictionDestination.DISCARD:
177	            self._l1_manager.delete(action.keys)
178	        else:
179	            logger.error("Unsupported eviction destination: %s", action.destination)
180	            logger.error("Treating it as DISCARD.")
181	            self._l1_manager.delete(action.keys)

184	class L2AdapterEvictionState:
185	    """Per-adapter eviction state: its own policy, listener, and config."""
186	
187	    def __init__(
188	        self,
189	        adapter_id: int,
190	        adapter: L2AdapterInterface,
191	        eviction_config: EvictionConfig,
192	    ):
193	        self.adapter_id = adapter_id
194	        self.adapter = adapter
195	        self.eviction_config = eviction_config
196	        self.eviction_policy = CreateEvictionPolicy(eviction_config)
197	        self.listener = L2EvictionPolicy(self.eviction_policy)
198	        adapter.register_listener(self.listener)

201	class L2EvictionController(StorageControllerInterface):
202	    """
203	    Unified eviction controller for all L2 adapters.
204	
205	    Each adapter gets its own eviction policy and listener bridge, but a
206	    single background thread loops over all of them.
207	
208	    When the adapter's policy sets ``support_isolation == True``
209	    (e.g. :class:`IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy`), the controller consults
210	    the injected :class:`QuotaManager` to decide which ``cache_salt``
211	    buckets are over budget and evicts from each one in isolation.
212	    Otherwise it uses the adapter's aggregate ``usage_fraction``
213	    against the configured watermark — unchanged from the pre-PR5
214	    behavior.
215	    """
216	
217	    def __init__(
218	        self,
219	        l2_adapter_states: list[L2AdapterEvictionState],
220	        quota_manager: QuotaManager | None = None,
221	    ):
222	        self._adapter_states = l2_adapter_states
223	        self._quota_manager = quota_manager
224	        # Guards _adapter_states against concurrent runtime add/remove.
225	        self._states_lock = threading.Lock()
226	        self._stop_flag = threading.Event()
227	        self._thread = threading.Thread(
228	            target=self._eviction_loop,
229	            daemon=True,
230	        )
231	
232	    def start(self):
233	        logger.info("Starting %s...", self.__class__.__name__)
234	        self._thread.start()
235	
236	    def stop(self):
237	        self._stop_flag.set()
238	        self._thread.join()
239	
240	    def add_adapter_state(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState) -> None:
241	        """Register a new adapter's eviction state at runtime."""
242	        with self._states_lock:
243	            self._adapter_states.append(state)
244	
245	    def remove_adapter_state(self, adapter_id: int) -> None:
246	        """Drop the eviction state for ``adapter_id``.
247	
248	        Blocks until any in-progress eviction pass finishes (it holds the
249	        same lock), so the adapter is guaranteed idle here before the
250	        caller closes it. A no-op if the adapter has no eviction state.
251	        """
252	        with self._states_lock:
253	            self._adapter_states = [
254	                s for s in self._adapter_states if s.adapter_id != adapter_id
255	            ]
256	
257	    def report_status(self) -> dict:
258	        # NOTE: ``usage.bytes_by_cache_salt`` is intentionally NOT
259	        # surfaced here. A deployment can have 10k+ salts, so embedding
260	        # the full bucket map in the status response would blow up the
261	        # payload. Per-salt inspection goes through the dedicated HTTP
262	        # quota endpoints (which pull from ``QuotaManager`` +
263	        # ``StorageManager.get_usage_bytes_by_cache_salt``).
264	        adapter_statuses = []
265	        with self._states_lock:
266	            states = list(self._adapter_states)
267	        for state in states:
268	            usage = state.adapter.get_usage()
269	            adapter_statuses.append(
270	                {
271	                    "eviction_policy": state.eviction_config.eviction_policy,
272	                    "trigger_watermark": state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark,
273	                    "eviction_ratio": state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio,
274	                    "current_usage": usage.usage_fraction,
275	                    "total_bytes_used": usage.total_bytes_used,
276	                    "total_capacity_bytes": usage.total_capacity_bytes,
277	                    "num_cache_salt_buckets": len(usage.bytes_by_cache_salt),
278	                }
279	            )
280	        return {
281	            "is_healthy": self._thread.is_alive(),
282	            "thread_alive": self._thread.is_alive(),
283	            "adapters": adapter_statuses,
284	        }
285	
286	    def _eviction_loop(self):
287	        while not self._stop_flag.is_set():
288	            time.sleep(1)
289	            # Hold the lock across the whole pass so remove_adapter_state
290	            # cannot detach (and the caller close) an adapter while we are
291	            # calling into it.
292	            with self._states_lock:
293	                for state in self._adapter_states:
294	                    self._check_and_evict(state)
295	
296	    def _check_and_evict(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
297	        if state.eviction_policy.support_isolation and self._quota_manager is not None:
298	            self._check_and_evict_by_cache_salt(state)
299	        else:
300	            self._check_and_evict_global(state)
301	
302	    def _check_and_evict_global(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
303	        """Aggregate-usage eviction (``LRU`` / ``noop``)."""
304	        watermark = state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark
305	        eviction_ratio = state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio
306	
307	        # ``usage_fraction == -1`` means the adapter doesn't support
308	        # usage-based eviction (no max_capacity_bytes declared), so we
309	        # do not trigger eviction. Adapters with ``supports_global_eviction ==
310	        # False`` should already have been filtered out at construction
311	        # time in ``StorageManager``; this check is a defensive belt.
312	        current_usage = state.adapter.get_usage().usage_fraction
313	        if current_usage < 0 or current_usage < watermark:
314	            logger.debug(
315	                "L2 usage %.2f below watermark %.2f; skipping eviction.",
316	                current_usage,
317	                watermark,
318	            )
319	            return
320	
321	        logger.info(
322	            "L2 usage %.2f above watermark %.2f; triggering eviction.",
323	            current_usage,
324	            watermark,
325	        )
326	        actions = state.eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(eviction_ratio)
327	        for action in actions:
328	            self._execute_eviction_action(state.adapter, action)
329	
330	    def _check_and_evict_by_cache_salt(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
331	        """Per-``cache_salt`` eviction driven by :class:`QuotaManager`.
332	
333	        For every salt with non-zero bytes, compare its usage against
334	        ``watermark * quota``. Salts over threshold get eviction scoped
335	        to their own LRU list. Salts with no quota registered have an
336	        effective limit of ``0`` and are therefore always over budget,
337	        so they get a full eviction (``effective_ratio=1.0``) — this
338	        enforces the allowlist rule: only registered salts retain data.
339	
340	        Per-destination keys are batched across all over-budget salts
341	        before invoking the adapter — one ``adapter.delete(...)`` call
342	        per destination instead of one per (salt, destination) pair.
343	        Adapters with non-trivial per-call overhead (NIXL handle setup,
344	        FS sync, etc.) see this as a real win when many salts go over
345	        budget in the same cycle.
346	        """
347	        assert self._quota_manager is not None
348	        watermark = state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark
349	        eviction_ratio = state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio
350	        usage = state.adapter.get_usage()
351	
352	        # destination -> accumulated keys across all over-budget salts.
353	        pending: dict[EvictionDestination, list[ObjectKey]] = {}
354	
355	        for cache_salt, user_bytes in usage.bytes_by_cache_salt.items():
356	            if user_bytes <= 0:
357	                continue
358	            limit = self._quota_manager.get_limit_bytes(cache_salt)
359	            # Trigger on ``>=`` to match the global branch's ``usage <
360	            # watermark`` short-circuit. Salts with no quota (limit=0)
361	            # always land here because ``user_bytes > 0 >= 0``.
362	            if user_bytes < watermark * limit:
363	                continue
364	
365	            # Unregistered / zero-quota salts: wipe everything.
366	            # Registered salts: evict the configured ratio of their list.
367	            effective_ratio = 1.0 if limit == 0 else eviction_ratio
368	            logger.info(
369	                "cache_salt=%r over quota (bytes=%d, limit=%d, "
370	                "watermark=%.2f); evicting ratio=%.2f.",
371	                cache_salt,
372	                user_bytes,
373	                limit,
374	                watermark,
375	                effective_ratio,
376	            )
377	            actions = state.eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(
378	                effective_ratio, cache_salt=cache_salt
379	            )
380	            for action in actions:
381	                pending.setdefault(action.destination, []).extend(action.keys)
382	
383	        for destination, keys in pending.items():
384	            self._execute_eviction_action(
385	                state.adapter,
386	                EvictionAction(keys=keys, destination=destination),
387	            )
388	
389	    def _execute_eviction_action(
390	        self, adapter: L2AdapterInterface, action: EvictionAction
391	    ):
392	        if action.destination == EvictionDestination.DISCARD:
393	            adapter.delete(action.keys)
394	        else:
395	            logger.error("Unsupported eviction destination: %s", action.destination)
396	            logger.error("Treating it as DISCARD.")
397	            adapter.delete(action.keys)
398	
399	        if action.keys:
400	            get_event_bus().publish(
401	                Event(
402	                    event_type=EventType.L2_KEYS_EVICTED,
403	                    metadata={
404	                        "key_count": len(action.keys),
405	                        "key_count_per_salt": Counter(
406	                            k.cache_salt for k in action.keys
407	                        ),
408	                    },
409	                )
410	            )

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

# Future
from __future__ import annotations

# Standard
from abc import abstractmethod
from collections import Counter
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
import threading
import time

# First Party
from lmcache.logging import init_logger
from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey
from lmcache.v1.distributed.config import EvictionConfig
from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction import L1EvictionPolicy, L2EvictionPolicy
from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction_policy import CreateEvictionPolicy
from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
    EvictionAction,
    EvictionDestination,
)
from lmcache.v1.distributed.l1_manager import L1Manager
from lmcache.v1.distributed.l2_adapters.base import L2AdapterInterface
from lmcache.v1.distributed.storage_controller import StorageControllerInterface
from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event import Event, EventType
from lmcache.v1.mp_observability.event_bus import get_event_bus

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    # First Party
    from lmcache.v1.distributed.quota_manager import QuotaManager

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class EvictionController(StorageControllerInterface):
    """
    Abstract base class for eviction controllers.

    Provides the shared eviction loop structure: background thread and stop
    flag. Subclasses implement eviction_loop and execute_eviction_action
    for their specific tier (L1 or L2).
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._stop_flag = threading.Event()
        self._thread = threading.Thread(
            target=self.eviction_loop,
            daemon=True,
        )

    def start(self):
        logger.info("Starting %s...", self.__class__.__name__)
        self._thread.start()

    def stop(self):
        self._stop_flag.set()
        self._thread.join()

    @abstractmethod
    def report_status(self) -> dict:
        """Return a status dict for this controller.

        The child class needs to override this function to report
        controller-specific health and configuration information.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def eviction_loop(self):
        """Run the eviction loop.

        The child class needs to override this function to implement
        internal eviction controlling logic.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def execute_eviction_action(self, action: EvictionAction):
        """Execute a single eviction action.

        The child class needs to override this function to implement
        internal eviction controlling logic.
        """
        pass


class L1EvictionController(EvictionController):
    """
    Eviction controller for L1 cache.

    Uses an L1EvictionPolicy bridge to keep the eviction policy up-to-date
    with L1 manager events, and periodically triggers eviction based on
    L1 memory usage.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        l1_manager: L1Manager,
        eviction_config: EvictionConfig,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self._eviction_config = eviction_config
        self._eviction_policy = CreateEvictionPolicy(eviction_config)
        self._l1_manager = l1_manager
        self._listener = L1EvictionPolicy(self._eviction_policy)
        self._l1_manager.register_listener(self._listener)
        self._event_bus = get_event_bus()

    def report_status(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "is_healthy": self._thread.is_alive(),
            "thread_alive": self._thread.is_alive(),
            "eviction_policy": self._eviction_config.eviction_policy,
            "trigger_watermark": self._eviction_config.trigger_watermark,
            "eviction_ratio": self._eviction_config.eviction_ratio,
        }

    def _publish_skipped(self, usage: float, watermark: float) -> None:
        """Publish a below-watermark loop tick (no eviction this cycle)."""
        self._event_bus.publish(
            Event(
                event_type=EventType.L1_EVICTION_LOOP_TICK,
                metadata={
                    "usage": usage,
                    "watermark": watermark,
                    "triggered": False,
                },
            )
        )

    def _publish_triggered(self, usage: float, watermark: float) -> None:
        """Publish an above-watermark loop tick (eviction policy ran)."""
        self._event_bus.publish(
            Event(
                event_type=EventType.L1_EVICTION_LOOP_TICK,
                metadata={
                    "usage": usage,
                    "watermark": watermark,
                    "triggered": True,
                },
            )
        )

    def eviction_loop(self):
        watermark = self._eviction_config.trigger_watermark
        eviction_ratio = self._eviction_config.eviction_ratio

        while not self._stop_flag.is_set():
            time.sleep(1)
            used_bytes, total_bytes = self._l1_manager.get_memory_usage()
            usage = 0 if total_bytes == 0 else used_bytes / total_bytes
            if usage < watermark:
                logger.debug(
                    "L1 memory usage %.2f below watermark %.2f; skipping eviction.",
                    usage,
                    watermark,
                )
                self._publish_skipped(usage, watermark)
                continue

            logger.info(
                "L1 memory usage %.2f above watermark %.2f; triggering eviction.",
                usage,
                watermark,
            )
            actions = self._eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(
                eviction_ratio,
                key_eligible_filter=self._l1_manager.is_key_evictable,
            )
            for action in actions:
                self.execute_eviction_action(action)
            self._publish_triggered(usage, watermark)

    def execute_eviction_action(self, action: EvictionAction):
        if action.destination == EvictionDestination.DISCARD:
            self._l1_manager.delete(action.keys)
        else:
            logger.error("Unsupported eviction destination: %s", action.destination)
            logger.error("Treating it as DISCARD.")
            self._l1_manager.delete(action.keys)


class L2AdapterEvictionState:
    """Per-adapter eviction state: its own policy, listener, and config."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        adapter_id: int,
        adapter: L2AdapterInterface,
        eviction_config: EvictionConfig,
    ):
        self.adapter_id = adapter_id
        self.adapter = adapter
        self.eviction_config = eviction_config
        self.eviction_policy = CreateEvictionPolicy(eviction_config)
        self.listener = L2EvictionPolicy(self.eviction_policy)
        adapter.register_listener(self.listener)


class L2EvictionController(StorageControllerInterface):
    """
    Unified eviction controller for all L2 adapters.

    Each adapter gets its own eviction policy and listener bridge, but a
    single background thread loops over all of them.

    When the adapter's policy sets ``support_isolation == True``
    (e.g. :class:`IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy`), the controller consults
    the injected :class:`QuotaManager` to decide which ``cache_salt``
    buckets are over budget and evicts from each one in isolation.
    Otherwise it uses the adapter's aggregate ``usage_fraction``
    against the configured watermark — unchanged from the pre-PR5
    behavior.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        l2_adapter_states: list[L2AdapterEvictionState],
        quota_manager: QuotaManager | None = None,
    ):
        self._adapter_states = l2_adapter_states
        self._quota_manager = quota_manager
        # Guards _adapter_states against concurrent runtime add/remove.
        self._states_lock = threading.Lock()
        self._stop_flag = threading.Event()
        self._thread = threading.Thread(
            target=self._eviction_loop,
            daemon=True,
        )

    def start(self):
        logger.info("Starting %s...", self.__class__.__name__)
        self._thread.start()

    def stop(self):
        self._stop_flag.set()
        self._thread.join()

    def add_adapter_state(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState) -> None:
        """Register a new adapter's eviction state at runtime."""
        with self._states_lock:
            self._adapter_states.append(state)

    def remove_adapter_state(self, adapter_id: int) -> None:
        """Drop the eviction state for ``adapter_id``.

        Blocks until any in-progress eviction pass finishes (it holds the
        same lock), so the adapter is guaranteed idle here before the
        caller closes it. A no-op if the adapter has no eviction state.
        """
        with self._states_lock:
            self._adapter_states = [
                s for s in self._adapter_states if s.adapter_id != adapter_id
            ]

    def report_status(self) -> dict:
        # NOTE: ``usage.bytes_by_cache_salt`` is intentionally NOT
        # surfaced here. A deployment can have 10k+ salts, so embedding
        # the full bucket map in the status response would blow up the
        # payload. Per-salt inspection goes through the dedicated HTTP
        # quota endpoints (which pull from ``QuotaManager`` +
        # ``StorageManager.get_usage_bytes_by_cache_salt``).
        adapter_statuses = []
        with self._states_lock:
            states = list(self._adapter_states)
        for state in states:
            usage = state.adapter.get_usage()
            adapter_statuses.append(
                {
                    "eviction_policy": state.eviction_config.eviction_policy,
                    "trigger_watermark": state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark,
                    "eviction_ratio": state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio,
                    "current_usage": usage.usage_fraction,
                    "total_bytes_used": usage.total_bytes_used,
                    "total_capacity_bytes": usage.total_capacity_bytes,
                    "num_cache_salt_buckets": len(usage.bytes_by_cache_salt),
                }
            )
        return {
            "is_healthy": self._thread.is_alive(),
            "thread_alive": self._thread.is_alive(),
            "adapters": adapter_statuses,
        }

    def _eviction_loop(self):
        while not self._stop_flag.is_set():
            time.sleep(1)
            # Hold the lock across the whole pass so remove_adapter_state
            # cannot detach (and the caller close) an adapter while we are
            # calling into it.
            with self._states_lock:
                for state in self._adapter_states:
                    self._check_and_evict(state)

    def _check_and_evict(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
        if state.eviction_policy.support_isolation and self._quota_manager is not None:
            self._check_and_evict_by_cache_salt(state)
        else:
            self._check_and_evict_global(state)

    def _check_and_evict_global(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
        """Aggregate-usage eviction (``LRU`` / ``noop``)."""
        watermark = state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark
        eviction_ratio = state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio

        # ``usage_fraction == -1`` means the adapter doesn't support
        # usage-based eviction (no max_capacity_bytes declared), so we
        # do not trigger eviction. Adapters with ``supports_global_eviction ==
        # False`` should already have been filtered out at construction
        # time in ``StorageManager``; this check is a defensive belt.
        current_usage = state.adapter.get_usage().usage_fraction
        if current_usage < 0 or current_usage < watermark:
            logger.debug(
                "L2 usage %.2f below watermark %.2f; skipping eviction.",
                current_usage,
                watermark,
            )
            return

        logger.info(
            "L2 usage %.2f above watermark %.2f; triggering eviction.",
            current_usage,
            watermark,
        )
        actions = state.eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(eviction_ratio)
        for action in actions:
            self._execute_eviction_action(state.adapter, action)

    def _check_and_evict_by_cache_salt(self, state: L2AdapterEvictionState):
        """Per-``cache_salt`` eviction driven by :class:`QuotaManager`.

        For every salt with non-zero bytes, compare its usage against
        ``watermark * quota``. Salts over threshold get eviction scoped
        to their own LRU list. Salts with no quota registered have an
        effective limit of ``0`` and are therefore always over budget,
        so they get a full eviction (``effective_ratio=1.0``) — this
        enforces the allowlist rule: only registered salts retain data.

        Per-destination keys are batched across all over-budget salts
        before invoking the adapter — one ``adapter.delete(...)`` call
        per destination instead of one per (salt, destination) pair.
        Adapters with non-trivial per-call overhead (NIXL handle setup,
        FS sync, etc.) see this as a real win when many salts go over
        budget in the same cycle.
        """
        assert self._quota_manager is not None
        watermark = state.eviction_config.trigger_watermark
        eviction_ratio = state.eviction_config.eviction_ratio
        usage = state.adapter.get_usage()

        # destination -> accumulated keys across all over-budget salts.
        pending: dict[EvictionDestination, list[ObjectKey]] = {}

        for cache_salt, user_bytes in usage.bytes_by_cache_salt.items():
            if user_bytes <= 0:
                continue
            limit = self._quota_manager.get_limit_bytes(cache_salt)
            # Trigger on ``>=`` to match the global branch's ``usage <
            # watermark`` short-circuit. Salts with no quota (limit=0)
            # always land here because ``user_bytes > 0 >= 0``.
            if user_bytes < watermark * limit:
                continue

            # Unregistered / zero-quota salts: wipe everything.
            # Registered salts: evict the configured ratio of their list.
            effective_ratio = 1.0 if limit == 0 else eviction_ratio
            logger.info(
                "cache_salt=%r over quota (bytes=%d, limit=%d, "
                "watermark=%.2f); evicting ratio=%.2f.",
                cache_salt,
                user_bytes,
                limit,
                watermark,
                effective_ratio,
            )
            actions = state.eviction_policy.get_eviction_actions(
                effective_ratio, cache_salt=cache_salt
            )
            for action in actions:
                pending.setdefault(action.destination, []).extend(action.keys)

        for destination, keys in pending.items():
            self._execute_eviction_action(
                state.adapter,
                EvictionAction(keys=keys, destination=destination),
            )

    def _execute_eviction_action(
        self, adapter: L2AdapterInterface, action: EvictionAction
    ):
        if action.destination == EvictionDestination.DISCARD:
            adapter.delete(action.keys)
        else:
            logger.error("Unsupported eviction destination: %s", action.destination)
            logger.error("Treating it as DISCARD.")
            adapter.delete(action.keys)

        if action.keys:
            get_event_bus().publish(
                Event(
                    event_type=EventType.L2_KEYS_EVICTED,
                    metadata={
                        "key_count": len(action.keys),
                        "key_count_per_salt": Counter(
                            k.cache_salt for k in action.keys
                        ),
                    },
                )
            )

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
Eviction module to determine what to evict from L1 and L2 caches.
"""

# Standard
from abc import abstractmethod
from collections.abc import Callable

# First Party
from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey
from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
    EvictionAction,
    EvictionDestination,
    L1ManagerListener,
    L2AdapterListener,
)


class EvictionPolicy:
    """
    Pure abstract base class for eviction policies.

    Subclasses implement the LRU (or other) tracking logic via the
    on_keys_* methods and expose eviction decisions via get_eviction_actions.
    The binding to a specific cache tier (L1 or L2) is provided by
    L1EvictionPolicy and L2EvictionPolicy respectively.
    """

    @property
    def support_isolation(self) -> bool:
        """Whether this policy supports isolation eviction (e.g., per user isolation).

        When True, the eviction controller checks isolated usage (e.g., per user usage)
        and passes ``cache_salt`` to ``get_eviction_actions()`` to scope
        eviction to specific cache_salt. When False, the controller uses
        aggregate usage only.

        Default is False. Subclasses that support isolated eviction.
        (e.g., ``IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy``) should override to return True.
        """
        return False

    @abstractmethod
    def register_eviction_destination(self, destination: EvictionDestination):
        """
        Register an eviction destination for the eviction policy to use.

        Args:
            destination (EvictionDestination): The eviction destination to
                register.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_keys_created(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the eviction policy that new keys have been created.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been created.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_keys_touched(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the eviction policy that keys have been accessed.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been accessed.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_keys_removed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the eviction policy that keys have been deleted.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been deleted.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def get_eviction_actions(
        self,
        expected_ratio: float,
        key_eligible_filter: Callable[[ObjectKey], bool] | None = None,
        cache_salt: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[EvictionAction]:
        """
        Get the eviction actions to evict objects from cache.

        Args:
            expected_ratio (float): A hint indicating approximately what
                fraction of tracked keys should be evicted. Value should be
                in range [0.0, 1.0]. For example, 0.1 means roughly 10% of
                keys should be evicted. This is a hint and the policy may
                return more or fewer keys.
            key_eligible_filter: An optional callable that takes an ObjectKey
                and returns True if the key is eligible for eviction. When
                provided, keys for which the filter returns False will be
                skipped. This is useful for skipping locked keys that
                cannot be deleted.
            cache_salt: When set, scope eviction to keys belonging to this
                salt only (identified by ``ObjectKey.cache_salt``). When
                None, evict globally across all salts. Only meaningful for
                policies where ``support_isolation`` is True; other policies
                ignore this parameter.

        Returns:
            list[EvictionAction]: The eviction actions to perform. Each
                action contains the keys and one eviction destination.

        Notes:
            The eviction action may not be successfully executed, or it may
            be executed asynchronously. Therefore, the eviction policy should
            not assume that the objects are evicted immediately, but it should
            use `on_keys_removed` to know when the objects are actually
            deleted.
        """
        pass


class L1EvictionPolicy(L1ManagerListener):
    """
    Bridges L1Manager lifecycle events to an EvictionPolicy instance.

    The actual eviction policy is provided via the constructor, keeping
    the policy logic decoupled from the listener interface.
    """

    def __init__(self, policy: EvictionPolicy):
        self._policy = policy

    @property
    def policy(self) -> EvictionPolicy:
        return self._policy

    # L1ManagerListener implementations
    def on_l1_keys_reserved_read(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        # No-op
        pass

    def on_l1_keys_read_finished(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_touched(keys)

    def on_l1_keys_reserved_write(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        # No-op
        pass

    def on_l1_keys_write_finished(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        # TODO (ApostaC): we don't differentiate between the created keys and
        # updated keys here. Probably need to fix that by introducing a new
        # callback in L1ManagerListener or adding `mode` argument into
        # on_keys_reserved_write.
        self._policy.on_keys_created(keys)

    def on_l1_keys_deleted_by_manager(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_removed(keys)

    def on_l1_keys_finish_write_and_reserve_read(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_created(keys)

    def on_l1_keys_accessed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_touched(keys)


class L2EvictionPolicy(L2AdapterListener):
    """
    Bridges L2Adapter lifecycle events to an EvictionPolicy instance.

    The actual eviction policy is provided via the constructor, keeping
    the policy logic decoupled from the listener interface.
    """

    def __init__(self, policy: EvictionPolicy):
        self._policy = policy

    @property
    def policy(self) -> EvictionPolicy:
        return self._policy

    def on_l2_keys_stored(self, keys: list[ObjectKey], sizes: list[int]):
        self._policy.on_keys_created(keys)

    def on_l2_keys_accessed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_touched(keys)

    def on_l2_keys_deleted(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        self._policy.on_keys_removed(keys)

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""
3	IsolatedLRU (per-``cache_salt`` LRU) eviction policy.
4	
5	Unlike :class:`LRUEvictionPolicy` which maintains a single global LRU
6	list, this policy keeps one LRU list per ``cache_salt`` and uses
7	``support_isolation=True`` so the L2 eviction controller can scope
8	eviction to a specific salt via the ``cache_salt`` argument to
9	``get_eviction_actions``.
10	
11	Combined with :class:`QuotaManager`, this lets the controller evict
12	only the over-quota ``cache_salt``'s keys while leaving other salts'
13	cached data untouched.
14	"""
15	
16	# Future
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	# Standard
20	from collections import OrderedDict
21	from collections.abc import Callable
22	import threading
23	
24	# First Party
25	from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey
26	from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction import EvictionPolicy
27	from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
28	    EvictionAction,
29	    EvictionDestination,
30	)
31	
32	
33	class IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy(EvictionPolicy):
34	    """Per-``cache_salt`` LRU eviction policy.
35	
36	    Maintains one ``OrderedDict`` per ``cache_salt`` (keyed by
37	    ``ObjectKey``). New and touched keys move to the end of their
38	    bucket's ordering; least-recently-used keys stay at the front and
39	    are evicted first.
40	
41	    Thread-safety: every public method holds a single lock so bucket
42	    dicts cannot be mutated concurrently.
43	    """
44	
45	    @property
46	    def support_isolation(self) -> bool:
47	        return True
48	
49	    def __init__(
50	        self,
51	        default_destination: EvictionDestination = EvictionDestination.DISCARD,
52	    ):
53	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
54	        # cache_salt -> ordered {ObjectKey: None} (oldest first).
55	        self._per_salt_order: dict[str, OrderedDict[ObjectKey, None]] = {}
56	        # Registered destinations (first one wins if any are registered,
57	        # matching LRUEvictionPolicy semantics).
58	        self._destinations: list[EvictionDestination] = []
59	        self._default_destination = default_destination
60	
61	    def register_eviction_destination(self, destination: EvictionDestination) -> None:
62	        with self._lock:
63	            if destination not in self._destinations:
64	                self._destinations.append(destination)
65	
66	    def on_keys_created(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
67	        if not keys:
68	            return
69	        with self._lock:
70	            # Same prefix-match ordering rationale as ``LRUEvictionPolicy``:
71	            # later keys in a request should be evicted first, so we
72	            # insert in reverse to place the final key at the LRU head.
73	            for key in reversed(keys):
74	                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
75	                if order is None:
76	                    order = OrderedDict()
77	                    self._per_salt_order[key.cache_salt] = order
78	                if key in order:
79	                    order.move_to_end(key)
80	                else:
81	                    order[key] = None
82	
83	    def on_keys_touched(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
84	        if not keys:
85	            return
86	        with self._lock:
87	            for key in reversed(keys):
88	                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
89	                if order is not None and key in order:
90	                    order.move_to_end(key)
91	
92	    def on_keys_removed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
93	        if not keys:
94	            return
95	        with self._lock:
96	            for key in keys:
97	                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
98	                if order is None:
99	                    continue
100	                order.pop(key, None)
101	                if not order:
102	                    # Drop the empty bucket so ``list``/iteration
103	                    # snapshots stay compact.
104	                    del self._per_salt_order[key.cache_salt]
105	
106	    def get_eviction_actions(
107	        self,
108	        expected_ratio: float,
109	        key_eligible_filter: Callable[[ObjectKey], bool] | None = None,
110	        cache_salt: str | None = None,
111	    ) -> list[EvictionAction]:
112	        """Select victims in LRU order, scoped to a single ``cache_salt``.
113	
114	        IsolatedLRU is "isolated only" by contract — callers must pass a
115	        concrete ``cache_salt``. The base interface keeps ``cache_salt``
116	        optional for compatibility with non-isolated policies; passing
117	        ``None`` here raises ``ValueError`` rather than silently
118	        falling back to a global pool.
119	
120	        Args:
121	            expected_ratio: Fraction of the candidate pool to evict,
122	                clamped to ``[0.0, 1.0]``. If the ratio rounds down to
123	                zero and the pool is non-empty, at least one key is
124	                returned (matches ``LRUEvictionPolicy``).
125	            key_eligible_filter: Optional predicate — keys failing the
126	                filter (e.g. locked) are skipped.
127	            cache_salt: The salt whose bucket to evict from. Required.
128	
129	        Returns:
130	            A list with at most one ``EvictionAction`` (one per
131	            destination — IsolatedLRU has a single destination, so the
132	            list is either empty or length 1). Empty when nothing is
133	            available to evict under the current filter / ratio.
134	
135	        Raises:
136	            ValueError: If ``cache_salt`` is ``None``.
137	        """
138	        if cache_salt is None:
139	            raise ValueError(
140	                "IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy.get_eviction_actions requires "
141	                "cache_salt; this policy is per-bucket and has no global "
142	                "eviction path."
143	            )
144	        with self._lock:
145	            order = self._per_salt_order.get(cache_salt)
146	            pool = list(order.keys()) if order else []
147	
148	            if not pool:
149	                return []
150	
151	            expected_ratio = max(0.0, min(1.0, expected_ratio))
152	            target_count = int(len(pool) * expected_ratio)
153	            if expected_ratio > 0 and target_count == 0:
154	                target_count = 1
155	            if target_count == 0:
156	                return []
157	
158	            keys_to_evict: list[ObjectKey] = []
159	            for key in pool:
160	                if key_eligible_filter is not None and not key_eligible_filter(key):
161	                    continue
162	                keys_to_evict.append(key)
163	                if len(keys_to_evict) >= target_count:
164	                    break
165	
166	            if not keys_to_evict:
167	                return []
168	
169	            destination = self._default_destination
170	            if self._destinations:
171	                destination = self._destinations[0]
172	
173	            return [EvictionAction(keys=keys_to_evict, destination=destination)]
174	
175	    # =========================================================================
176	    # Methods below are NOT part of the EvictionPolicy interface.
177	    # They are provided for testing and debugging purposes only.
178	    # =========================================================================
179	
180	    def get_num_tracked_keys(self, cache_salt: str | None = None) -> int:
181	        """Number of tracked keys, optionally scoped to one bucket."""
182	        with self._lock:
183	            if cache_salt is not None:
184	                order = self._per_salt_order.get(cache_salt)
185	                return len(order) if order is not None else 0
186	            return sum(len(o) for o in self._per_salt_order.values())
187	
188	    def get_tracked_salts(self) -> list[str]:
189	        """Return the set of cache_salts with at least one tracked key."""
190	        with self._lock:
191	            return list(self._per_salt_order.keys())
192

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
IsolatedLRU (per-``cache_salt`` LRU) eviction policy.

Unlike :class:`LRUEvictionPolicy` which maintains a single global LRU
list, this policy keeps one LRU list per ``cache_salt`` and uses
``support_isolation=True`` so the L2 eviction controller can scope
eviction to a specific salt via the ``cache_salt`` argument to
``get_eviction_actions``.

Combined with :class:`QuotaManager`, this lets the controller evict
only the over-quota ``cache_salt``'s keys while leaving other salts'
cached data untouched.
"""

# Future
from __future__ import annotations

# Standard
from collections import OrderedDict
from collections.abc import Callable
import threading

# First Party
from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey
from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction import EvictionPolicy
from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
    EvictionAction,
    EvictionDestination,
)


class IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy(EvictionPolicy):
    """Per-``cache_salt`` LRU eviction policy.

    Maintains one ``OrderedDict`` per ``cache_salt`` (keyed by
    ``ObjectKey``). New and touched keys move to the end of their
    bucket's ordering; least-recently-used keys stay at the front and
    are evicted first.

    Thread-safety: every public method holds a single lock so bucket
    dicts cannot be mutated concurrently.
    """

    @property
    def support_isolation(self) -> bool:
        return True

    def __init__(
        self,
        default_destination: EvictionDestination = EvictionDestination.DISCARD,
    ):
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        # cache_salt -> ordered {ObjectKey: None} (oldest first).
        self._per_salt_order: dict[str, OrderedDict[ObjectKey, None]] = {}
        # Registered destinations (first one wins if any are registered,
        # matching LRUEvictionPolicy semantics).
        self._destinations: list[EvictionDestination] = []
        self._default_destination = default_destination

    def register_eviction_destination(self, destination: EvictionDestination) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            if destination not in self._destinations:
                self._destinations.append(destination)

    def on_keys_created(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
        if not keys:
            return
        with self._lock:
            # Same prefix-match ordering rationale as ``LRUEvictionPolicy``:
            # later keys in a request should be evicted first, so we
            # insert in reverse to place the final key at the LRU head.
            for key in reversed(keys):
                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
                if order is None:
                    order = OrderedDict()
                    self._per_salt_order[key.cache_salt] = order
                if key in order:
                    order.move_to_end(key)
                else:
                    order[key] = None

    def on_keys_touched(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
        if not keys:
            return
        with self._lock:
            for key in reversed(keys):
                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
                if order is not None and key in order:
                    order.move_to_end(key)

    def on_keys_removed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]) -> None:
        if not keys:
            return
        with self._lock:
            for key in keys:
                order = self._per_salt_order.get(key.cache_salt)
                if order is None:
                    continue
                order.pop(key, None)
                if not order:
                    # Drop the empty bucket so ``list``/iteration
                    # snapshots stay compact.
                    del self._per_salt_order[key.cache_salt]

    def get_eviction_actions(
        self,
        expected_ratio: float,
        key_eligible_filter: Callable[[ObjectKey], bool] | None = None,
        cache_salt: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[EvictionAction]:
        """Select victims in LRU order, scoped to a single ``cache_salt``.

        IsolatedLRU is "isolated only" by contract — callers must pass a
        concrete ``cache_salt``. The base interface keeps ``cache_salt``
        optional for compatibility with non-isolated policies; passing
        ``None`` here raises ``ValueError`` rather than silently
        falling back to a global pool.

        Args:
            expected_ratio: Fraction of the candidate pool to evict,
                clamped to ``[0.0, 1.0]``. If the ratio rounds down to
                zero and the pool is non-empty, at least one key is
                returned (matches ``LRUEvictionPolicy``).
            key_eligible_filter: Optional predicate — keys failing the
                filter (e.g. locked) are skipped.
            cache_salt: The salt whose bucket to evict from. Required.

        Returns:
            A list with at most one ``EvictionAction`` (one per
            destination — IsolatedLRU has a single destination, so the
            list is either empty or length 1). Empty when nothing is
            available to evict under the current filter / ratio.

        Raises:
            ValueError: If ``cache_salt`` is ``None``.
        """
        if cache_salt is None:
            raise ValueError(
                "IsolatedLRUEvictionPolicy.get_eviction_actions requires "
                "cache_salt; this policy is per-bucket and has no global "
                "eviction path."
            )
        with self._lock:
            order = self._per_salt_order.get(cache_salt)
            pool = list(order.keys()) if order else []

            if not pool:
                return []

            expected_ratio = max(0.0, min(1.0, expected_ratio))
            target_count = int(len(pool) * expected_ratio)
            if expected_ratio > 0 and target_count == 0:
                target_count = 1
            if target_count == 0:
                return []

            keys_to_evict: list[ObjectKey] = []
            for key in pool:
                if key_eligible_filter is not None and not key_eligible_filter(key):
                    continue
                keys_to_evict.append(key)
                if len(keys_to_evict) >= target_count:
                    break

            if not keys_to_evict:
                return []

            destination = self._default_destination
            if self._destinations:
                destination = self._destinations[0]

            return [EvictionAction(keys=keys_to_evict, destination=destination)]

    # =========================================================================
    # Methods below are NOT part of the EvictionPolicy interface.
    # They are provided for testing and debugging purposes only.
    # =========================================================================

    def get_num_tracked_keys(self, cache_salt: str | None = None) -> int:
        """Number of tracked keys, optionally scoped to one bucket."""
        with self._lock:
            if cache_salt is not None:
                order = self._per_salt_order.get(cache_salt)
                return len(order) if order is not None else 0
            return sum(len(o) for o in self._per_salt_order.values())

    def get_tracked_salts(self) -> list[str]:
        """Return the set of cache_salts with at least one tracked key."""
        with self._lock:
            return list(self._per_salt_order.keys())

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
No-op eviction policy for buffer-only mode.

When L1 is used purely as a write buffer (data is deleted from L1
immediately after being stored to L2 by the StoreController), there
is no need for the eviction policy to track keys at all.
"""

# Standard
from collections.abc import Callable

# First Party
from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey
from lmcache.v1.distributed.eviction import EvictionPolicy
from lmcache.v1.distributed.internal_api import (
    EvictionAction,
    EvictionDestination,
)


class NoOpEvictionPolicy(EvictionPolicy):
    """
    Eviction policy that performs no tracking and never evicts.

    Designed for buffer-only mode where the StoreController is
    responsible for cleaning up L1 after successful L2 writes.
    This avoids the overhead of maintaining an LRU OrderedDict
    that would otherwise do pointless insert-then-remove cycles.
    """

    def register_eviction_destination(self, destination: EvictionDestination):
        pass

    def on_keys_created(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        pass

    def on_keys_touched(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        pass

    def on_keys_removed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        pass

    def get_eviction_actions(
        self,
        expected_ratio: float,
        key_eligible_filter: Callable[[ObjectKey], bool] | None = None,
        cache_salt: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[EvictionAction]:
        return []

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""
Class for distributed storage manager internal API data structures
"""

# Standard
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import enum

# First Party
from lmcache.v1.distributed.api import ObjectKey


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class L1MemoryDesc:
    """
    Describes the L1 memory buffer registered with an external backend (e.g. Nixl).
    """

    ptr: int
    size: int
    align_bytes: int


class EventListener(ABC):  # noqa: B024
    pass


# For L1 manager event notifications
class L1ManagerListener(EventListener):
    """
    Listener for L1 manager events
    """

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_reserved_read(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that new keys have been reserved for read on L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been successfully reserved
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_read_finished(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been accessed on L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been successfully read
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_reserved_write(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been reserved for write on L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been successfully reserved
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_write_finished(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been finished for writing on L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been successfully written
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_finish_write_and_reserve_read(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been finished for writing
        and reserved for read on L1.

        This will only be trigger by the prefetch operation now.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been successfully
                finished for writing and reserved for read
        """
        # NOTE (ApostaC): may consider renaming this to `on_l1_keys_finish_prefetch`
        # for better clarity
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_deleted_by_manager(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been deleted from L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been deleted
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l1_keys_accessed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been accessed on L1.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been accessed
        """
        pass


class L2AdapterListener(EventListener):
    """Listener for L2 adapter events, analogous to L1ManagerListener."""

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l2_keys_stored(self, keys: list[ObjectKey], sizes: list[int]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been successfully stored in L2.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been stored.
            sizes (list[int]): The byte size of each stored key.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l2_keys_accessed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been accessed (lookup hit) in L2.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been accessed.
        """
        pass

    @abstractmethod
    def on_l2_keys_deleted(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]):
        """
        Notify the listener that keys have been deleted from L2.

        Args:
            keys (list[ObjectKey]): The keys that have been deleted.
        """
        pass


# For Eviction
class EvictionDestination(enum.Enum):
    """
    The destination of evicted objects
    """

    DISCARD = enum.auto()
    """Discard the evicted objects"""

    L2_CACHE = enum.auto()
    """Evict to L2 storage"""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class EvictionAction:
    """
    An action to be taken for eviction
    """

    destination: EvictionDestination
    """The destination of the evicted object"""

    keys: list[ObjectKey] = field(default_factory=list)
    """The key of the object to be evicted"""


class L2StoreResult(int):
    """Immutable result of a completed L2 store task.

    Encodes both the success flag and bytes transferred in the int
    value: ``>= 0`` means success (value = bytes transferred);
    ``-1`` means failure.

    Args:
        success: Whether the store task succeeded.
        bytes_transferred: Bytes actually written to L2. Must be >= 0.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If ``bytes_transferred`` is negative.
    """

    def __new__(cls, success: bool, bytes_transferred: int) -> "L2StoreResult":
        if bytes_transferred < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"bytes_transferred must be >= 0, got {bytes_transferred}")
        return super().__new__(cls, bytes_transferred if success else -1)

    def is_successful(self) -> bool:
        """Return ``True`` when the store task succeeded."""
        return int(self) >= 0

    def bytes_transferred(self) -> int:
        """Return the number of bytes actually written, or 0 on failure."""
        value = int(self)
        return value if value >= 0 else 0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class QuotaEntry:
    """Snapshot of a single quota registration."""

    cache_salt: str
    limit_bytes: int

1	# L2 Adapter Eviction Design
2	
3	This document describes the eviction mechanism for L2 adapters: how eviction is
4	configured, how the eviction controller tracks key lifecycle events, and how
5	adapters participate in the eviction loop.
6	
7	## Overview
8	
9	L2 eviction is **per-adapter** and **opt-in**. Each L2 adapter instance can
10	independently declare an eviction policy via its JSON config. Adapters without
11	an `"eviction"` key in their config are excluded from the eviction loop.
12	
13	A single `L2EvictionController` manages all adapters that have eviction enabled.
14	Its background thread loops over every adapter each cycle, checking usage and
15	triggering eviction independently per adapter.
16	
17	## Architecture
18	
19	```
20	StorageManager
21	  │
22	  └─ L2EvictionController  (single instance, single thread)
23	       │
24	       ├─ L2AdapterEvictionState[0]
25	       │     adapter ──► L2Adapter[0]   (eviction_config set)
26	       │     policy  ──► EvictionPolicy
27	       │     listener ─► L2EvictionPolicy (bridge)
28	       │
29	       ├─ (L2Adapter[1] has no eviction config → not tracked)
30	       │
31	       └─ L2AdapterEvictionState[1]
32	             adapter ──► L2Adapter[2]   (eviction_config set)
33	             policy  ──► EvictionPolicy
34	             listener ─► L2EvictionPolicy (bridge)
35	```
36	
37	Each adapter with eviction enabled gets an `L2AdapterEvictionState` that bundles:
38	
39	- An **`EvictionPolicy`** instance (e.g., LRU) that tracks key state.
40	- An **`L2EvictionPolicy`** listener bridge registered on the adapter. The
41	  bridge translates adapter events into policy `on_keys_*` calls via
42	  composition (no multi-inheritance).
43	
44	The controller and each adapter communicate through two channels:
45	
46	1. **Listener callbacks** — the `L2EvictionPolicy` bridge is registered as an
47	   `L2AdapterListener` on the adapter. The adapter fires events when keys are
48	   stored, accessed, or deleted, and the bridge forwards them to the eviction
49	   policy to keep its key tracking up-to-date.
50	
51	2. **`delete(keys)`** — when the eviction policy decides to evict, the
52	   controller calls `adapter.delete(keys)` directly. The adapter removes those
53	   keys from its storage and fires an `on_l2_keys_deleted` callback, which the
54	   bridge forwards to the policy so it removes them from its tracking state.
55	
56	## Configuration
57	
58	Eviction is configured as an optional `"eviction"` sub-object in each adapter's
59	JSON spec passed to `--l2-adapter`:
60	
61	```json
62	{
63	  "type": "mock",
64	  "max_size_gb": 10,
65	  "mock_bandwidth_gb": 4,
66	  "eviction": {
67	    "eviction_policy": "LRU",
68	    "trigger_watermark": 0.8,
69	    "eviction_ratio": 0.2
70	  }
71	}
72	```
73	
74	| Field               | Type    | Default | Description                                                     |
75	|---------------------|---------|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
76	| `eviction_policy`   | string  | —       | Policy name: `"LRU"` or `"noop"`. Required.                    |
77	| `trigger_watermark` | float   | `0.8`   | Usage fraction [0, 1] above which eviction is triggered.        |
78	| `eviction_ratio`    | float   | `0.2`   | Fraction of **used** capacity to evict each cycle.              |
79	
80	If the `"eviction"` key is absent, no `L2AdapterEvictionState` is created for
81	that adapter instance and it is excluded from the eviction loop.
82	
83	The eviction config is parsed by `L2AdapterConfigBase._parse_eviction_config()`
84	and stored as `adapter_config.eviction_config: EvictionConfig | None`.
85	
86	## Key Components
87	
88	### `L2AdapterListener` (`internal_api.py`)
89	
90	Abstract interface for receiving L2 adapter events:
91	
92	```python
93	class L2AdapterListener:
94	    def on_l2_keys_stored(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]): ...
95	    def on_l2_keys_accessed(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]): ...
96	    def on_l2_keys_deleted(self, keys: list[ObjectKey]): ...
97	```
98	
99	### Listener Infrastructure in `L2AdapterInterface` (`l2_adapters/base.py`)
100	
101	The base class owns the listener list and provides:
102	
103	- `register_listener(listener)` — adds a listener; called by
104	  `L2AdapterEvictionState.__init__`.
105	- `_notify_keys_stored(keys)` / `_notify_keys_accessed(keys)` /
106	  `_notify_keys_deleted(keys)` — protected helpers that fan out to all
107	  registered listeners. Adapter implementations call these after mutating
108	  their storage.
109	
110	No per-adapter code is needed to support listeners — just call
111	`super().__init__()` and use the `_notify_*` helpers.
112	
113	### `L2AdapterEvictionState` (`storage_controllers/eviction_controller.py`)
114	
115	Bundles the per-adapter eviction state: the adapter reference, its
116	`EvictionConfig`, an `EvictionPolicy` instance, and an `L2EvictionPolicy`
117	listener bridge. On construction, it registers the bridge on the adapter:
118	
119	```python
120	L2AdapterEvictionState(adapter, eviction_config)
121	  → creates EvictionPolicy from config
122	  → creates L2EvictionPolicy(policy)   # listener bridge
123	  → adapter.register_listener(bridge)  # subscribe to adapter events
124	```
125	
126	### `L2EvictionPolicy` (`eviction.py`)
127	
128	Listener bridge that inherits only `L2AdapterListener` and delegates events
129	to an `EvictionPolicy` via composition:
130	
131	| Callback               | Delegates to              |
132	|------------------------|---------------------------|
133	| `on_l2_keys_stored`    | `policy.on_keys_created`  |
134	| `on_l2_keys_accessed`  | `policy.on_keys_touched`  |
135	| `on_l2_keys_deleted`   | `policy.on_keys_removed`  |
136	
137	### `L2EvictionController` (`storage_controllers/eviction_controller.py`)
138	
139	A single controller that manages all adapters with eviction enabled. It owns
140	one background thread and a list of `L2AdapterEvictionState` objects.
141	
142	**Eviction loop:**
143	
144	Every second, the thread iterates over all adapter states. For each adapter,
145	it calls `adapter.get_usage()` which returns
146	`(current_usage, usage_after_ongoing_eviction)`. If `current_usage` exceeds
147	that adapter's `trigger_watermark`, the policy's
148	`get_eviction_actions(eviction_ratio)` is called and the resulting keys are
149	passed to `adapter.delete()`.
150	
151	### Eviction Policy (`eviction_policy/`)
152	
153	The eviction policy is a pure data structure — it tracks keys and decides which
154	to evict. It has no knowledge of adapters or listeners:
155	
156	```
157	EvictionPolicy (abstract)
158	  ├─ LRUEvictionPolicy   — evicts least-recently-used keys
159	  └─ NoOpEvictionPolicy  — never evicts
160	```
161	
162	Policies are created by `CreateEvictionPolicy(eviction_config)` in
163	`eviction_policy/factory.py`.
164	
165	## Adapter Implementation Guide
166	
167	To support eviction in a new adapter:
168	
169	1. **Call `super().__init__()`** in the adapter's `__init__`. This initializes
170	   the listener list from the base class.
171	
172	2. **Fire `_notify_keys_stored(keys)`** after keys are durably written to L2
173	   storage (e.g., after a store task completes).
174	
175	3. **Fire `_notify_keys_deleted(keys)`** inside `delete()` after keys are
176	   removed. Only fire for keys that were actually removed — skip keys not found
177	   and keys skipped due to pinning.
178	
179	4. **Fire `_notify_keys_accessed(keys)`** when a lookup or load marks a key as
180	   recently used (optional — improves LRU accuracy).
181	
182	5. **Implement `delete(keys)`** to remove keys from storage. Pinned keys (in use
183	   by an in-flight load) should be skipped; the eviction controller will retry
184	   them on the next cycle.
185	
186	6. **Implement `get_usage() -> (current, projected)`** to return the current
187	   storage utilization as a fraction in [0, 1]. `projected` may equal `current`
188	   if in-flight deletions are not tracked.
189	
190	Adapters that do not support eviction (e.g., a remote adapter with unbounded
191	capacity) can omit steps 2–6 and rely on the base class no-op defaults.
192	
193	## Adapter Support Matrix
194	
195	| Adapter                    | `delete` | `get_usage` | Listener events     |
196	|----------------------------|----------|-------------|---------------------|
197	| `MockL2Adapter`            | ✓        | ✓           | stored, deleted     |
198	| `NixlStoreL2Adapter`       | ✓ (skips pinned) | ✓ (pool-based) | stored, deleted |
199	| `RawBlockL2Adapter`        | ✓ (skips locked) | ✓ | stored, accessed, deleted |
200	| `FSL2Adapter`              | no-op    | `(-1, -1)`  | none                |
201	| `NativeConnectorL2Adapter` | ✓ (via `submit_batch_delete`) | ✓ (client-side, requires `max_capacity_gb`) | stored, deleted |
202	
203	**Note on `NativeConnectorL2Adapter`:** Eviction support requires two things:
204	
205	1. The underlying C++ connector must implement `do_single_delete()` (built-in Redis
206	   and FS connectors do; third-party plugins may not — in which case `delete()` is a
207	   no-op).
208	2. The adapter must be configured with `max_capacity_gb > 0` to enable client-side
209	   size tracking for `get_usage()`. Without it, `get_usage()` returns `(-1, -1)` and
210	   the eviction controller will not trigger.
211	
212	Example configuration with eviction enabled:
213	
214	```json
215	{
216	  "type": "resp",
217	  "host": "localhost",
218	  "port": 6379,
219	  "max_capacity_gb": 10,
220	  "eviction": {
221	    "eviction_policy": "LRU",
222	    "trigger_watermark": 0.8,
223	    "eviction_ratio": 0.2
224	  }
225	}
226	```
227	
228	## Data Flow: Eviction Cycle
229	
230	```
231	[Background thread — every 1s]
232	  │
233	  ▼
234	for each L2AdapterEvictionState:
235	  │
236	  ▼
237	  state.adapter.get_usage()
238	    → (current_usage, _)
239	    │
240	    ├─ current_usage < watermark → skip this adapter
241	    │
242	    └─ current_usage ≥ watermark
243	         │
244	         ▼
245	    state.policy.get_eviction_actions(eviction_ratio)
246	         → list[EvictionAction(keys, destination=DISCARD)]
247	         │
248	         ▼
249	    state.adapter.delete(eviction_action.keys)
250	         │
251	         ├─ removes keys from storage
252	         └─ calls _notify_keys_deleted(deleted_keys)
253	              │
254	              ▼
255	         L2EvictionPolicy bridge → policy.on_keys_removed
256	              → updates internal tracking (e.g., LRU order)
257	```
258	
259	## Relationship to L1 Eviction
260	
261	L1 and L2 eviction share the same policy classes (`LRUEvictionPolicy`,
262	`NoOpEvictionPolicy`) and the same listener-bridge pattern (composition over
263	multi-inheritance). They differ in how they are wired:
264	
265	| Aspect              | L1                                   | L2                                    |
266	|---------------------|--------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
267	| Controller          | `L1EvictionController`               | `L2EvictionController`                |
268	| Listener bridge     | `L1EvictionPolicy`                   | `L2EvictionPolicy`                    |
269	| Listener interface  | `L1ManagerListener`                  | `L2AdapterListener`                   |
270	| Usage source        | `L1Manager.get_memory_usage()`       | `L2AdapterInterface.get_usage()`      |
271	| Config location     | `StorageManagerConfig.eviction_config` | `L2AdapterConfigBase.eviction_config` |
272	| Cardinality         | One per `StorageManager`             | One controller for all adapters       |
273	| Created by          | `StorageManager.__init__`            | `StorageManager.__init__`             |
274
